title: Validating Git Ref Names for Forbidden Characters

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate if a given Git ref name is valid according to the following rules: The name must not contain any forbidden characters (i.e., ", /, <, >, |, *, ?, {, }, [, ], `). The name must be at least 1 character long. The name must not exceed 200 characters in length. The name must not start or end with a slash (/). The function should return True if the name is valid, False otherwise. You will be given a string as input, which represents the ref name.
statement.notes: Note that Git ref names can be alphanumeric, dot, hyphen, or underscore.

examples.explanation: Invalid ref name (ends with a slash)
Invalid ref name (starts with a slash)
Invalid ref name (ends with a slash)

hints.text: First hint text: Check for forbidden characters. Use a regular expression to validate the input string.

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.intuition: Explanation of the correct approach: Regular expressions can be used to match patterns in strings.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach summary: Manual iteration through the string to check for each forbidden character.
editorial.betterSolution: Regular expression can be used to match the pattern.
editorial.optimalSolution: Optimal solution details: Use the following regular expression to validate the name.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Correctness proof: The regular expression will match the pattern if it's valid and not match otherwise.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Check the regular expression against the examples.
commonMistakes: Mistake 1: Using a regular expression that matches the pattern instead of one that doesn't match it.
Mistake 2: Not checking for the length of the string.
